mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth with 20-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or if negative RSAFS growth is followed by positive returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 5.0%, the labor market signals overheating, prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods with UNRATE below 5.0%, SPX 5-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above respective thresholds indicate rising inflation and aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity prices, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If in the combined elevated condition SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During Federal Reserve tightening cycles defined by a sequence of rate hikes, the cumulative increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) raises the risk-free rate and discount factor, reducing present value of future cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased by at least 25 basis points in the past 3 months
failure_condition: If in the 20 trading days following a 25bp or more increase in FEDFUNDS over 3 months, SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of episodes, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect expanding output and capacity utilization, signaling strong economic activity that lifts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month growth with 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or if negative INDPRO growth is followed by positive returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 5% month-over-month signals a weakening residential investment cycle and broader economic slowdown, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If after a month-over-month HOUST decline greater than 5%, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals a broad-based contraction in the labor market and consumer spending, indicating recessionary conditions that reduce corporate profits and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If in months where both PAYEMS and RSAFS decline, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of above-trend economic growth where real GDP (GDPC1) growth exceeds 4% year-over-year and industrial production (INDPRO) exceeds 5% year-over-year, supply constraints emerge that fuel cost pressures and inflation, leading the Federal Reserve to tighten policy and depressing S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth > 4% and INDPRO year-over-year growth > 5%
failure_condition: If when both GDPC1 and INDPRO growth exceed their thresholds, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05).
